paragraphs.source: By Node, Chip, and Device; Apple’s Wafer Economics at TSMC
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: ### By Node, Chip, and Device; Apple’s Wafer Economics at TSMC
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: By Node, Chip, and Device; Apple’s Wafer Economics at TSMC

pro.text: 按制程节点、芯片与设备划分：苹果在台积电的晶圆经济账

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s wafer demand at TSMC grew 3.2x between 2014 and 2025, translating to an 11% CAGR. But that topline figure obscures the actual journey. The growth was a staircase.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s wafer demand at TSMC grew 3.2x between 2014 and 2025, translating to an 11% CAGR. But that topline figure obscures the actual journey. The growth was a staircase.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple’s wafer demand at TSMC grew 3.2x between 2014 and 2025, translating to an 11% CAGR. But that topline figure obscures the actual journey. The growth was a staircase.

pro.text: 2014 至 2025 年间，苹果在台积电的晶圆需求增长 3.2 倍，复合年增长率（CAGR）达 11%。但这组宏观数据掩盖了实际的发展轨迹。其增长呈现阶梯式跃升。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: From 2014 to 2017, demand grew steadily at around 10% annually as 16nm and 20nm ramped alongside iPhone volumes. Then growth stalled. Between 2018 and 2019, total wafer demand went sideways even as N7 entered production. Apple was digesting the prior node transition before the next leg could begin.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: From 2014 to 2017, demand grew steadily at around 10% annually as 16nm and 20nm ramped alongside iPhone volumes. Then growth stalled. Between 2018 and 2019, total wafer demand went sideways even as N7 entered production. Apple was digesting the prior node transition before the next leg could begin.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: From 2014 to 2017, demand grew steadily at around 10% annually as 16nm and 20nm ramped alongside iPhone volumes. Then growth stalled. Between 2018 and 2019, total wafer demand went sideways even as N7 entered production. Apple was digesting the prior node transition before the next leg could begin.

pro.text: 2014 至 2017 年间，随着 16nm 和 20nm 伴随 iPhone 销量爬坡，需求以每年约 10% 的速度稳步增长。随后增长停滞。2018 至 2019 年间，尽管 N7 进入量产阶段，总晶圆需求却横盘整理。苹果正在消化上一次节点转换，蓄力开启下一轮增长。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: The real inflection came in 2020. Demand surged 37% in a single year as N5 ramped and Apple Silicon launched for Mac. The following two years sustained that momentum, with demand nearly doubling between 2019 and 2022. This was the steepest slope in the entire dataset, and it fundamentally changed Apple’s silicon footprint.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The real inflection came in 2020. Demand surged 37% in a single year as N5 ramped and Apple Silicon launched for Mac. The following two years sustained that momentum, with demand nearly doubling between 2019 and 2022. This was the steepest slope in the entire dataset, and it fundamentally changed Apple’s silicon footprint.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The real inflection came in 2020. Demand surged 37% in a single year as N5 ramped and Apple Silicon launched for Mac. The following two years sustained that momentum, with demand nearly doubling between 2019 and 2022. This was the steepest slope in the entire dataset, and it fundamentally changed Apple’s silicon footprint.

pro.text: 真正的拐点出现在 2020 年。随着 N5 产能爬坡以及 Mac 搭载苹果自研芯片发布，晶圆需求在单年内飙升 37%。随后两年延续了这一强劲势头，2019 至 2022 年间需求几乎翻倍。这是整个数据集中最陡峭的增长曲线，并从根本上重塑了苹果的芯片版图。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: Then came deceleration. In 2024, Apple posted its first year-over-year volume decline since 2013. N5 collapsed faster than N3 could absorb, and total demand contracted roughly 10%. But 2025 marks a sharp rebound as N3 peaks and N2 enters production.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Then came deceleration. In 2024, Apple posted its first year-over-year volume decline since 2013. N5 collapsed faster than N3 could absorb, and total demand contracted roughly 10%. But 2025 marks a sharp rebound as N3 peaks and N2 enters production.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Then came deceleration. In 2024, Apple posted its first year-over-year volume decline since 2013. N5 collapsed faster than N3 could absorb, and total demand contracted roughly 10%. But 2025 marks a sharp rebound as N3 peaks and N2 enters production.

pro.text: 紧接着便是增速放缓。2024 年，苹果录得自 2013 年以来的首次需求同比下滑。N5 需求崩盘的速度超过 N3 的承接能力，导致总需求萎缩约 10%。但随着 N3 达到峰值且 N2 投入量产，2025 年将迎来强势反弹。

paragraphs.source: The key insight is structural: Apple’s wafer demand moves in stair-steps, with multi-year plateaus followed by sudden jumps. Every jump aligns with a major node transition.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The key insight is structural: Apple’s wafer demand moves in stair-steps, with multi-year plateaus followed by sudden jumps. Every jump aligns with a major node transition.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The key insight is structural: Apple’s wafer demand moves in stair-steps, with multi-year plateaus followed by sudden jumps. Every jump aligns with a major node transition.

pro.text: 核心洞察在于结构层面：苹果的晶圆需求呈阶梯式演进，数年的平台期后往往伴随猛烈跃升。每一次跃升都与重大的节点转换高度契合。

pro.text: 制程节点主导地位日益强化

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s share of each new node at launch tells a striking story. On 20nm in 2014, Apple drove 77% of node revenue at peak. On 16nm in 2016, the peak was 46%. On N7 in 2018, it was 47%. Then N5 arrived, and Apple captured 75% of node revenue in 2021. On N3 in 2023, Apple’s share hit 92%.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s share of each new node at launch tells a striking story. On 20nm in 2014, Apple drove 77% of node revenue at peak. On 16nm in 2016, the peak was 46%. On N7 in 2018, it was 47%. Then N5 arrived, and Apple captured 75% of node revenue in 2021. On N3 in 2023, Apple’s share hit 92%.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple’s share of each new node at launch tells a striking story. On 20nm in 2014, Apple drove 77% of node revenue at peak. On 16nm in 2016, the peak was 46%. On N7 in 2018, it was 47%. Then N5 arrived, and Apple captured 75% of node revenue in 2021. On N3 in 2023, Apple’s share hit 92%.

pro.text: 苹果在每个新节点发布初期的份额占比，揭示了一个惊人的趋势。2014 年在 20nm 节点上，苹果在巅峰期贡献了该节点 77% 的营收。2016 年在 16nm 节点上，这一峰值为 46%。2018 年在 N7 节点上为 47%。随后 N5 问世，苹果在 2021 年攫取了该节点 75% 的营收。到了 2023 年的 N3 节点，苹果的份额更是高达 92%。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。

paragraphs.source: That last number is extraordinary. Apple funded nearly the entire yield learning curve for TSMC’s most advanced node. By the time other customers reached volume, Apple had already extracted the premium economics. The trend line is convex: Apple is capturing progressively larger shares of each new node at launch.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: That last number is extraordinary. Apple funded nearly the entire yield learning curve for TSMC’s most advanced node. By the time other customers reached volume, Apple had already extracted the premium economics. The trend line is convex: Apple is capturing progressively larger shares of each new node at launch.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: That last number is extraordinary. Apple funded nearly the entire yield learning curve for TSMC’s most advanced node. By the time other customers reached volume, Apple had already extracted the premium economics. The trend line is convex: Apple is capturing progressively larger shares of each new node at launch.

pro.text: 最后一个数字堪称不可思议。苹果几乎以一己之力，承担了台积电最先进制程节点整个良率学习曲线的资金成本。等到其他客户实现规模量产时，苹果早已充分榨取了丰厚的早期经济红利。这一趋势线呈凸型增长（前置爆发）：在每个新节点发布初期，苹果攫取的份额正持续攀升。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: TSMC Share Volatility
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: ### TSMC Share Volatility
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: TSMC Share Volatility

pro.text: 占台积电营收份额的波动

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。

paragraphs.source: Apple’s share of total TSMC revenue moved within a band of 17% to 24% over the decade. Apple’s share spikes when it leads a new node ramp, then fades as other customers scale, then spikes again at the next transition.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Apple’s share of total TSMC revenue moved within a band of 17% to 24% over the decade. Apple’s share spikes when it leads a new node ramp, then fades as other customers scale, then spikes again at the next transition.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Apple’s share of total TSMC revenue moved within a band of 17% to 24% over the decade. Apple’s share spikes when it leads a new node ramp, then fades as other customers scale, then spikes again at the next transition.

pro.text: 过去十年间，苹果占台积电总营收的份额一直在 17% 至 24% 的区间内波动。当苹果主导新节点产能爬坡时，其份额会急剧飙升；随着其他客户扩大规模，该份额随之回落；到了下一次节点转换时，份额又会再次飙升。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: The 2023 peak of 24% coincided with N3’s launch year. The 2025 trough of 17% reflects AI accelerator customers absorbing leading-edge capacity. NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscaler ASICs grew faster than Apple during this window. Apple’s absolute spend continued rising, but its relative share shrank because TSMC’s total revenue grew even faster.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The 2023 peak of 24% coincided with N3’s launch year. The 2025 trough of 17% reflects AI accelerator customers absorbing leading-edge capacity. NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscaler ASICs grew faster than Apple during this window. Apple’s absolute spend continued rising, but its relative share shrank because TSMC’s total revenue grew even faster.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The 2023 peak of 24% coincided with N3’s launch year. The 2025 trough of 17% reflects AI accelerator customers absorbing leading-edge capacity. NVIDIA, AMD, and hyperscaler ASICs grew faster than Apple during this window. Apple’s absolute spend continued rising, but its relative share shrank because TSMC’s total revenue grew even faster.

pro.text: 2023 年 24% 的峰值与 N3 的发布年份重合。2025 年 17% 的谷值则反映出 AI 加速器客户正在吸纳先进制程产能。在此期间，英伟达、AMD 和超大规模云服务商的 ASIC (专用集成电路) 增速超越了苹果。尽管苹果的绝对支出仍在持续攀升，但由于台积电总营收增速更快，其相对份额随之缩水。

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。
“went sideways”在金融或商业图表中意为“持平、没有明显增长或下降”。译为“横盘震荡”多了一层“上下波动（震荡）”的含义。

paragraphs.source: If we remove the 2020 outlier from the CAGR calculation, the underlying growth rate drops from 11% to closer to 8%. That single year, driven by N5 ramp plus COVID-fuelled Apple Silicon demand, inflates the topline number. While the growth trend is there, the magnitude is overstated by one exceptional year.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: If we remove the 2020 outlier from the CAGR calculation, the underlying growth rate drops from 11% to closer to 8%. That single year, driven by N5 ramp plus COVID-fuelled Apple Silicon demand, inflates the topline number. While the growth trend is there, the magnitude is overstated by one exceptional year.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: If we remove the 2020 outlier from the CAGR calculation, the underlying growth rate drops from 11% to closer to 8%. That single year, driven by N5 ramp plus COVID-fuelled Apple Silicon demand, inflates the topline number. While the growth trend is there, the magnitude is overstated by one exceptional year.

pro.text: 如果我们在计算复合年增长率（CAGR）时剔除 2020 年这一异常值，实际底层增长率将从 11% 降至接近 8%。在 N5 产能爬坡以及疫情激发的苹果自研芯片需求双重驱动下，单单这一年就拉高了整体增长数据。尽管增长趋势依然存在，但这一特殊年份夸大了实际的增长幅度。

paragraphs.source: N2: The Most Diversified Node Launch in TSMC History
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: ### N2: The Most Diversified Node Launch in TSMC History
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: N2: The Most Diversified Node Launch in TSMC History

pro.text: N2：台积电史上最多元化的节点发布

issues.description: 译文中出现了大段未翻译的英文原文（从'<50% of N2 demand...'一直到'...capturing >90%'），属于严重的漏译和格式检查失误。
“underlying growth rate”译为“潜在增长率”易与宏观经济学中的“potential growth rate”混淆；此外，“topline number”此处指代前文段落 1 提到的“11%复合年增长率”这一总体数据，译为“整体营收数据”无中生有了“营收”概念（原文通篇讨论的是晶圆需求量 wafer demand）。

paragraphs.source: N2 marks a structural shift in TSMC’s customer mix at node launch. We estimate Apple will once again the table with high share in N2 in 2026, but that represents <50% of N2 demand. Qualcomm (Snapdragon 8 Elite 2), AMD (Venice and MI450x), MediaTek (Dimensity 9600), Broadcom (ASIC), and Intel (Nova Lake) will follow. This is unprecedented. Previous leading-edge nodes were mobile-heavy, with Apple capturing >90% at peak. N2 splits demand across smartphones, PCs, servers, and networking from day one. HPC now drives capacity allocation, and Apple faces real competition for leading-edge capacity for the first time in a decade.

pro.text: N2 标志着台积电在节点发布初期的客户结构发生了结构性转变。我们预计，2026 年苹果将再次以高份额占据 N2 的主导地位，但这仅代表 <50% of N2 demand. Qualcomm (Snapdragon 8 Elite 2), AMD (Venice and MI450x), MediaTek (Dimensity 9600), Broadcom (ASIC), and Intel (Nova Lake) will follow. This is unprecedented. Previous leading-edge nodes were mobile-heavy, with Apple capturing >90% 的份额。而 N2 从发布首日起，就将需求分散至智能手机、PC、服务器和网络设备领域。如今，HPC 驱动着产能分配，苹果十年来首次在先进制程产能上面临真正的竞争。

Previous leading-edge nodes were mobile-heavy, with Apple capturing >]]]90% 的份额。而 N2 从发布首日起，就将需求分散至智能手机、PC、服务器和网络设备领域。如今，HPC 驱动着产能分配，苹果十年来首次在先进制程产能上面临真正的竞争。
